name: Autonomous Region of Bougainville (AROB)
summary: The Autonomous Region of Bougainville (AROB), comprising Bougainville Island + Buka Island + outlying islands, is an Oceanian Melanesian mixed common-law/Adat-customary-law autonomous region of Papua New Guinea — structurally distinctive globally as the world's most decisive independence referendum result in modern history (97.7% YES for independence in Bougainville Independence Referendum 23 November - 7 December 2019, ~181,067 YES out of ~185,272 valid votes — highest YES-percentage of any modern independence referendum globally, ahead of 2020 Bougainville-equivalent jurisdictions), as the only Papua New Guinea autonomous region established via formal peace-agreement framework derived from internal armed conflict resolution (Bougainville Peace Agreement 30 August 2001 ending the Bougainville Civil War 1988-1998 between Bougainville Revolutionary Army BRA and Papua New Guinea Defence Force PNGDF with ~15,000-20,000 deaths — proportionally one of the deadliest modern Oceanian internal armed conflicts), as the central jurisdiction of the most economically distinctive modern Oceanian internal armed conflict catalysed by Rio Tinto-operated Panguna Copper Mine framework (Panguna Mine operated 1972-1989 — closed 1989 due to BRA insurgency, representing ~17% of Papua New Guinea export revenue and ~44% of Papua New Guinea export revenue at peak, never reopened post-Civil War), and as the only modern independence-bound jurisdiction subject to formal post-referendum constitutional implementation framework (post-2019 referendum + 2021 Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation framework + 2027 target independence transition framework — Papua New Guinea Parliament 2027-target ratification framework). Bougainville comprises ~9,318 km² with a population of ~300,000. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Papua New Guinea common-law framework + Bougainville-specific Customary Court Adat-customary-law family-law framework for indigenous Melanesian communities. The Bougainville High Court is the apex regional court; the National Court of Papua New Guinea + Supreme Court of Papua New Guinea provide intermediate + final appellate jurisdiction. Bougainville is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Papua New Guinea is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Bougainville is governed by the PNG non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.title: Bougainville Peace Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: Agreement of 30 August 2001 between Papua New Guinea Government + Bougainville Revolutionary Army BRA + Bougainville Resistance Force BRF + other parties ending Bougainville Civil War 1988-1998 — providing for Bougainville Autonomous Government + Bougainville Independence Referendum framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of Papua New Guinea — Bougainville Special Provisions
statutory_framework.title: PNG Constitution — Bougainville Provisions
statutory_framework.relevance: Papua New Guinea Constitutional amendments 2002 implementing Bougainville Peace Agreement — providing for Bougainville Autonomous Government + Bougainville Independence Referendum framework.

statutory_framework.title: Bougainville Constitution
statutory_framework.relevance: Bougainville Constitution adopted 2004 — establishing Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG) framework.

statutory_framework.title: Bougainville Independence Referendum
statutory_framework.relevance: Bougainville Independence Referendum 23 November - 7 December 2019 — 97.7% YES for independence — world's most decisive modern independence referendum result.

statutory_framework.title: Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation Framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation framework established 2021 — 2027 target independence transition framework.

apex_courts.name: National Court of Papua New Guinea (Bougainville Circuit)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional trial court for civil and criminal matters from Bougainville.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Papua New Guinea
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Bougainville.

professional_regulators.name: Papua New Guinea Medical Board
professional_regulators.role: Papua New Guinea Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Bougainville.

anonymisation_convention: Bougainville family-court decisions are anonymised per Papua New Guinea court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Panguna Mine begins operations
key_developments.description: Rio Tinto-operated Panguna Copper Mine operations begin 1972 — ~17-44% of Papua New Guinea export revenue 1972-1989.

key_developments.title: Bougainville incorporated into PNG independence
key_developments.description: Bougainville incorporated into newly-independent Papua New Guinea on 16 September 1975 — initial unilateral declaration of independence by Bougainville on 1 September 1975 superseded by PNG independence.

key_developments.title: Bougainville Civil War begins
key_developments.description: Bougainville Civil War 1988-1998 — Bougainville Revolutionary Army BRA insurgency catalysed by Panguna Mine framework + cumulative ~15,000-20,000 deaths.

key_developments.title: Panguna Mine closes
key_developments.description: Panguna Copper Mine closes 1989 due to BRA insurgency — never reopened post-Civil War.

key_developments.title: Bougainville Civil War ceasefire
key_developments.description: Bougainville Civil War ceasefire 30 April 1998 — Lincoln Agreement framework.

key_developments.title: Bougainville Peace Agreement
key_developments.description: Bougainville Peace Agreement signed 30 August 2001 — providing for Bougainville Autonomous Government + Bougainville Independence Referendum framework.

key_developments.title: PNG Constitutional amendments
key_developments.description: Papua New Guinea Constitutional amendments 2002 implementing Bougainville Peace Agreement.

key_developments.title: Bougainville Constitution adopted
key_developments.description: Bougainville Constitution adopted 2004 — establishing Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG).

key_developments.title: Autonomous Bougainville Government established
key_developments.description: Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG) formally established 15 June 2005 — first ABG President Joseph Kabui.

key_developments.title: Bougainville Independence Referendum
key_developments.description: Bougainville Independence Referendum 23 November - 7 December 2019 — 97.7% YES for independence (181,067 YES of 185,272 valid votes).

key_developments.title: Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation Framework
key_developments.description: Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation framework established 2021 — 2027 target independence transition framework.

structural_findings: Bougainville operates a mixed federal Papua New Guinea common-law + Bougainville Adat-customary-law autonomous region framework — places Bougainville in the Oceanian Melanesian autonomous-region cluster.
World's most decisive independence referendum result in modern history (97.7% YES) is structurally distinctive globally — Bougainville Independence Referendum 2019.

Only Papua New Guinea autonomous region established via formal peace-agreement framework derived from internal armed conflict resolution is structurally distinctive globally — Bougainville Peace Agreement 2001 implementing 2001-onwards framework.

Central jurisdiction of most economically distinctive modern Oceanian internal armed conflict catalysed by Rio Tinto-operated Panguna Copper Mine framework is structurally distinctive globally — Panguna Mine 1972-1989 representing ~17-44% PNG export revenue.

Only modern independence-bound jurisdiction subject to formal post-referendum constitutional implementation framework is structurally distinctive globally — 2019 referendum + 2021 Joint Consultation + 2027 target independence transition framework.
Bougainville Civil War 1988-1998 with ~15,000-20,000 deaths is structurally distinctive globally — proportionally one of deadliest modern Oceanian internal armed conflicts.
Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG) framework established 2005 is structurally distinctive within Oceanian autonomous-region cluster.
editorial_notes: Bougainville jurisdiction sidecar — mixed federal Papua New Guinea common-law/Bougainville Adat-customary-law Oceanian Melanesian autonomous region (Bougainville Peace Agreement 2001 + PNG Constitution Bougainville Provisions 2002 + Bougainville Constitution 2004 + Bougainville Independence Referendum 2019 97.7% YES + Bougainville-PNG Joint Consultation Framework 2021+ + 2027 target independence transition + Panguna Mine 1972-1989 + Bougainville Civil War 1988-1998 + ABG established 2005 + PNG non-Hague-1980). World's most decisive independence referendum result in modern history globally + only PNG autonomous region established via formal peace-agreement framework + central jurisdiction of most economically distinctive modern Oceanian internal armed conflict + only modern independence-bound jurisdiction subject to formal post-referendum constitutional implementation framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Oceanian-Melanesian + mixed PNG-common-law/Bougainville-Adat-customary-law + PNG-autonomous-region cluster + Bougainville-Independence-Referendum-2019-97-7-percent-YES-globally-distinctive + Bougainville-Peace-Agreement-2001 + Bougainville-Civil-War-1988-1998 + Panguna-Copper-Mine-1972-1989 + ABG-2005 + 2027-target-independence-transition + PNG-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
